subject: DevOps
title: Load Balancers
description: A quick, practical guide to Load Balancers.

hook.text: One server handles 100 req/sec. You need 1,000. A load balancer spreads traffic across ten servers.

buildup.text: Load balancers distribute requests across backends. If one fails, traffic shifts to healthy ones.

discovery.text: Layer 4 routes by IP and port. Layer 7 routes by URL path, header, or cookie. Choose your level.

twist.text: Cloud load balancers are managed. AWS ALB and GCP HTTP LB handle millions of requests, zero ops.

climax.text: Health checks verify backends. Unhealthy servers leave the pool. Users never hit a broken node.

punchline.text: Load balancers: distribute, heal, and scale. Essential plumbing.

quiz.question: What is the difference between Layer 4 and Layer 7 load balancing?
options: Layer 4 routes by IP/port; Layer 7 routes by HTTP content like URLs and headers
Layer 4 is faster; Layer 7 is more secure
There is no meaningful difference between them
